home.positioning: Product engineer, not a feature engineer. I take ambiguous problems to production, now with AI in the stack.
home.bio: My edge isn't 'building apps.' It's finding where complex products break in practice and turning ambiguous problems into things that survive production. Six-plus years doing that solo; now I build the AI layer too: prompts, evals, retrieval, and the product around them.

strengths.title: AI features, 0→1
strengths.body: Prompts, evals, retrieval, and the product around them. Claude, RAG, and local models.

strengths.title: Production discipline
strengths.body: Typecheck→test, observability, CI. Code that survives real users, not just the demo.

strengths.title: End-to-end ownership
strengths.body: One surface, mobile to backend to infra. I build the machinery, not just the features.

hiring-radar.title: Hybrid search that doubles recall
hiring-radar.outcome: Lexical + vector ranking surfaces strong candidates that pure semantic search misses.

shoebox.title: On-device receipt OCR at 90% of cloud
shoebox.outcome: Local OCR + a quantized LLM reach ~90% of frontier cloud vision, offline and at $0.

rubric.title: CI for prompts
rubric.outcome: Golden sets + a calibrated LLM judge fail the PR when prompt quality regresses.

settle.title: AI bill review with a human in the loop
settle.outcome: Claude vision extracts and reconciles every line item before a human signs off.

today-only.title: A to-do app that only manages today
today-only.outcome: No backlog, no carry-over. Just Must Do vs Bonus, and 'do it now' under five minutes.

how-i-build-with-ai.title: My Claude Code workflow
how-i-build-with-ai.outcome: How I orchestrate agents and MCP tooling to ship production code at IOHK.

daily-news.title: daily-news · RAG inside a mobile app
daily-news.sub: Ask-the-News with local embeddings + Groq: grounded, cited answers in a Flutter app.

tether.title: Tether WDK wallet challenge
tether.sub: Non-custodial mobile wallet with biometric unlock and Secure-Enclave key storage.

offline.title: Offline-first posts app
offline.sub: Queued offline actions that auto-sync the moment the connection returns.

about.title: Product engineer, not a feature engineer, now shipping the AI layer end to end.
about.intro: I'm a product engineer, not a feature engineer. Most devs close the ticket and move on; I stay with how the thing actually feels: the timing, the edge case, the line nobody reads. That fixation isn't decoration. It's respect for whoever has to use what I ship. I take problems from ambiguous to in-production end to end: data model and APIs, the frontend, the infra, the tests, and now the AI layer (prompts, evals, retrieval, and the product around them).
about.howIntro: I build the machinery that produces features, not just the features. I work best as the technical owner of a surface, deciding where deterministic code ends and a model begins.

practice.title: Monorepos, one type system
practice.body: mobile, web and backend share types, so a contract changes in exactly one place.

practice.title: Deterministic gates
practice.body: typecheck, lint, test, build. Non-negotiable, before anything ships.

practice.title: Observability from day one
practice.body: error tables and honest UI states, not a black-box 'unknown' bolted on later.

practice.title: Provider-agnostic AI
practice.body: free and local by default, frontier when it earns it, with the trade-off measured (shoebox: ~90% of cloud at $0).

practice.title: Humans in the loop by design
practice.body: the model surfaces probability; a person decides where trust actually matters.

iohk.name: Input Output (IOHK)
iohk.body: On Lace, the multi-chain wallet, I architected a contract-module system that lets new chains and dApp integrations slot in without touching the core. Separately, I built the team's Claude Code framework: the agents and tooling other engineers now use day to day.

bolster.name: Bolster
bolster.body: Joined as the fourth engineer and built greenfield fintech surfaces from zero. I owned the core mobile experience as it grew to 100K+ users in seven months, then went back and made it fast and stable, cutting cold-start time and crashes hard.

globant.name: Globant
globant.body: Full-stack engineer on Lessen, a US proptech platform. I built the shared React Native component library that the product teams built on, standardizing the UI and cutting cross-platform effort by roughly a third.

moony.name: Moony
moony.body: Co-founded and built the product end to end: every layer, every decision. The crash course in owning a thing from idea to shipped that everything since has built on.

design.title: I design what I ship.
design.intro: Product engineering is my focus, but I design the products too: I work out the visual direction and a real design system in Claude Design, then rebuild it in this exact codebase (Next.js 16, Tailwind v4) and ship it. The hard part was never the mockup; it's making it survive a real codebase.
design.systemsNote: Each product runs on a real system I built in Claude Design, then shipped in code. Open one to see the whole thing: tokens, type, and components.
design.completeNote: Foundations plus every component, rendered straight from the Claude Design handoff.

hiring-radar.name: hiring-radar
hiring-radar.tagline: Mission-control system for a job-hunt radar: phosphor on near-black, data-dense, mono numerics.

settle.name: settle
settle.tagline: Money-dense system for an AP bill-pay product: light, calm, clarity over flash when dollars are on the line.

portfolio.name: Nico Mastakas
portfolio.tagline: The system behind this site: six colors, one accent, a 4px scale. Ported token-for-token into the CSS you are reading.

work.notTranslated: Showing the original version of this case study.

footer.positioning: Product engineer shipping AI features end to end.
